title: SEMI JOIN INTERSECT ANTI JOIN produces incorrect result
description:
### What happens?

`SEMI JOIN` and `ANTI JOIN` are opposite operations, so `SEMI JOIN INTERSECT ANTI JOIN` should produce an empty set. However, the following cases produce incorrect results (non-empty set).

### To Reproduce

```sql
-- When inserting two identical values into t0, one random value into t1, these bugs can be triggered.
-- AAB pattern: B is a random value, which can also be the same as A
CREATE TABLE t0(c0 INT);
CREATE TABLE t1(c0 INT);
INSERT INTO t0(c0) VALUES (0), (0);
INSERT INTO t1(c0) VALUES (1);

-- =, !=, <>, >, <=
-- These are some negative cases
(SELECT * FROM t0 SEMI JOIN t1 ON t0.rowid = t1.rowid) INTERSECT (SELECT * FROM t0 ANTI JOIN t1 ON t0.rowid = t1.rowid);
(SELECT * FROM t0 SEMI JOIN t1 ON t0.rowid != t1.rowid) INTERSECT (SELECT * FROM t0 ANTI JOIN t1 ON t0.rowid != t1.rowid);
(SELECT * FROM t0 SEMI JOIN t1 ON t0.rowid <> t1.rowid) INTERSECT (SELECT * FROM t0 ANTI JOIN t1 ON t0.rowid <> t1.rowid);
(SELECT * FROM t0 SEMI JOIN t1 ON t0.rowid > t1.rowid) INTERSECT (SELECT * FROM t0 ANTI JOIN t1 ON t0.rowid > t1.rowid);
(SELECT * FROM t0 SEMI JOIN t1 ON t0.rowid <= t1.rowid) INTERSECT (SELECT * FROM t0 ANTI JOIN t1 ON t0.rowid <= t1.rowid);
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ  c0   в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ int32 в”Ӯ
в”ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Ө
в”Ӯ   0   в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ

-- >=, <
-- These are some positive cases
(SELECT * FROM t0 SEMI JOIN t1 ON t0.rowid < t1.rowid) INTERSECT (SELECT * FROM t0 ANTI JOIN t1 ON t0.rowid < t1.rowid);
(SELECT * FROM t0 SEMI JOIN t1 ON t0.rowid >= t1.rowid) INTERSECT (SELECT * FROM t0 ANTI JOIN t1 ON t0.rowid >= t1.rowid);
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ   c0   в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ int32  в”Ӯ
в”ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Ө
в”Ӯ 0 rows в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
```

```sql
-- When inserting two identical values into t0, two random values into t1, these bugs can be triggered.
-- AABC pattern: B, C are random values, which can also be the same value as A
-- inserting two, four, six, or any even number of random values into t1 also triggers it
CREATE TABLE t0(c0 INT);
CREATE TABLE t1(c0 INT);
INSERT INTO t0(c0) VALUES (0), (0); 
INSERT INTO t1(c0) VALUES (1), (2);

-- >, <
-- These are some negative cases
(SELECT * FROM t0 SEMI JOIN t1 ON t0.rowid < t1.rowid) INTERSECT (SELECT * FROM t0 ANTI JOIN t1 ON t0.rowid < t1.rowid);
(SELECT * FROM t0 SEMI JOIN t1 ON t0.rowid > t1.rowid) INTERSECT (SELECT * FROM t0 ANTI JOIN t1 ON t0.rowid > t1.rowid);
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ  c0   в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ int32 в”Ӯ
в”ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Ө
в”Ӯ   0   в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ

-- =, !=, <>, >=, <=
-- These are some positive cases
(SELECT * FROM t0 SEMI JOIN t1 ON t0.rowid = t1.rowid) INTERSECT (SELECT * FROM t0 ANTI JOIN t1 ON t0.rowid = t1.rowid);
(SELECT * FROM t0 SEMI JOIN t1 ON t0.rowid != t1.rowid) INTERSECT (SELECT * FROM t0 ANTI JOIN t1 ON t0.rowid != t1.rowid);
(SELECT * FROM t0 SEMI JOIN t1 ON t0.rowid <> t1.rowid) INTERSECT (SELECT * FROM t0 ANTI JOIN t1 ON t0.rowid <> t1.rowid);
(SELECT * FROM t0 SEMI JOIN t1 ON t0.rowid >= t1.rowid) INTERSECT (SELECT * FROM t0 ANTI JOIN t1 ON t0.rowid >= t1.rowid);
(SELECT * FROM t0 SEMI JOIN t1 ON t0.rowid <= t1.rowid) INTERSECT (SELECT * FROM t0 ANTI JOIN t1 ON t0.rowid <= t1.rowid);
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ   c0   в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ int32  в”Ӯ
в”ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Ө
в”Ӯ 0 rows в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
```

```sql
-- When inserting two identical values into t0, two random values into t1, these bugs can be triggered.
-- AABCD pattern: B, C, D are random values, which can also be the same value as A
-- inserting three, five, seven, or any odd number of random values into t1 also triggers it
CREATE TABLE t0(c0 INT);
CREATE TABLE t1(c0 INT);
INSERT INTO t0(c0) VALUES (0), (0); 
INSERT INTO t1(c0) VALUES (1), (2), (3);

-- >
-- This is a negative case
(SELECT * FROM t0 SEMI JOIN t1 ON t0.rowid > t1.rowid) INTERSECT (SELECT * FROM t0 ANTI JOIN t1 ON t0.rowid > t1.rowid);

в”Ӯ   0   в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ

-- >, =, !=, <>, >=, <=
-- These are some positive cases
(SELECT * FROM t0 SEMI JOIN t1 ON t0.rowid < t1.rowid) INTERSECT (SELECT * FROM t0 ANTI JOIN t1 ON t0.rowid < t1.rowid);
(SELECT * FROM t0 SEMI JOIN t1 ON t0.rowid = t1.rowid) INTERSECT (SELECT * FROM t0 ANTI JOIN t1 ON t0.rowid = t1.rowid);
(SELECT * FROM t0 SEMI JOIN t1 ON t0.rowid != t1.rowid) INTERSECT (SELECT * FROM t0 ANTI JOIN t1 ON t0.rowid != t1.rowid);
(SELECT * FROM t0 SEMI JOIN t1 ON t0.rowid <> t1.rowid) INTERSECT (SELECT * FROM t0 ANTI JOIN t1 ON t0.rowid <> t1.rowid);
(SELECT * FROM t0 SEMI JOIN t1 ON t0.rowid >= t1.rowid) INTERSECT (SELECT * FROM t0 ANTI JOIN t1 ON t0.rowid >= t1.rowid);
(SELECT * FROM t0 SEMI JOIN t1 ON t0.rowid <= t1.rowid) INTERSECT (SELECT * FROM t0 ANTI JOIN t1 ON t0.rowid <= t1.rowid);
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ

в”Ӯ int32  в”Ӯ
```

bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE t0(c0 INT, c1 TEXT);
CREATE TABLE t1(c0 INT, c2 REAL);
SELECT * FROM t0 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM t1 WHERE t0.c0 = t1.c0) INTERSECT SELECT * FROM t0 WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM t1 WHERE t0.c0 = t1.c0);
key_bug_features: SEMI JOIN INTERSECT ANTI JOIN produces non-empty set
